Another example that came up for me just to, so anything just graduated recently from the sprint, and we had a bit of a safe motion that had to happen, which was not anything to do with what anyone did in the sprint, but there's a couple conflating factors.

One, the CEO was not a consistent participant, so he missed a lot of the handover between the sprint and his new EM, but what I'd ask for you guys to listen to Signals, because when I went back and listened to the call recordings, a couple things were clear.
One is, he kept threading in new agencies, so new people kept popping in to our channels, and that to me signaled a couple things.

One is, okay, he all of a sudden has a bunch of vendors playing in this space, all who want dollars, and secondly, it became clear that he had some very aggressive financial results to his board, and he did not meet them.
So, thinking about this young CEO who's trying to build super fast, I can just...
hear what happened to him in that board meeting, and it did not go well.

Why are you so expensive?
What are you doing for me?

So the signals for us to listen to in the sprint are, if you start hearing a bunch of external parties being brought in, that is a clear sign of, our differentiation, our what we're doing, what is our moat, and why are we sort of the thought leader in this process, I think is something for us just to listen for, establish, and then let the pod team know that there's a lot of players in the space.

And then, listen, you know, we should be curious about not just the results we're driving, but the results that are coming up for the stakeholder in the business, so that we understand any pressures, and also can support the leader in any talking points that will help them.

But his approach, like, he is very, like, open to discussion and just, like, talking about best practices.

He is not someone, like, I don't feel, he doesn't read as a person.
